prompt: Using ProteinMPNN-guided ancestral sequence reconstruction, infer and design ancestral RuBisCO large subunit (RbcL) sequences along the cyanobacteria-to-land-plant lineage using a maximum-likelihood phylogenetic tree of 500 modern RbcL sequences from NCBI RefSeq. Select ancestral nodes at ~500 Ma and ~250 Ma and generate ProteinMPNN sequences constrained to the ancestral backbone predicted by RoseTTAFold. outline a methodology covering: (1) multiple sequence alignment with MUSCLE and phylogenetic reconstruction with IQ-TREE, (2) ancestral state reconstruction with FastML at internal nodes, (3) backbone generation for ancestral sequences with ESMFold, (4) ProteinMPNN sequence sampling around ancestral backbone, (5) biochemical validation of ancestral CO2-fixation kinetics. Discuss implications for engineering improved CO2 fixation in synthetic biology.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and NCBI RefSeq; target: RuBisCO large subunit; organism: cyanobacteria/plant lineage; catalogue version v1.0
